MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; ***Court Reporter Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Sep-03-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STANDARD 5-10-25 CENT STORES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Stephen Rose, Esq. (415-332-6000; stephenkentrose@gmail.com) for Plaintiff.
Jason Shane, Esq. (925-705-7358; jbs@mhfirm.law) of McCaffery Hosking LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Standard 5-10-25 Cent Stores, LLC's summary judgment/adjudication motion is denied in full.

Plaintiff Doris Harris, 85, fell and broke a knee cap in one of defendant's stores. The accident occurred when plaintiff encountered a plastic crate (defendant calls it a "tote") on the floor of a narrow aisle, blocking half of it. (Memo. p. 3; PSS p. 9.) Defendant argues that, because Harris acknowledges having seen the crate, she cannot recover for premises lability or negligence. The "dangerous condition was open and obvious" excusing a duty to warn, defendant says, and "necessity did not require" plaintiff to "encounter" it.

For many decades, California courts have held that whether a dangerous condition is sufficiently open and obvious to excuse a business's duty to warn is a question of fact for the jury. (Chance v. Lawry's, Inc. (1962) 58 Cal.2d 368, 374; Beauchamp v. Los Gatos Golf Course (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 20, 33; Summer J. v. United States Baseball Federation (2020) 45 Cal.App.5th 261, 275.) This is particularly so where, as here, plaintiff says (and it could in any event be readily inferred) that she did not appreciate the obstacle's danger. (Beauchamp, 273 Cal.App.2d at 34-35; PSS pp. 4, 9.) Moreover, a "duty to remedy" may exist even where there may be no duty to warn of a dangerous condition. (Osborn v. Mission Ready Mix (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 104, 122.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
